book_name: Technical Analysis of the Financial Markets by John J. Murphy

entry_conditions: Buy when the price closes above a Simple Moving Average (SMA)...
Buy when a shorter-term moving average crosses over a longer-term moving average...
Sell when a shorter-term moving average crosses under a longer-term moving average...
Use buy stop orders to enter long positions automatically...
To trade a false breakout, enter in the direction of the initial breakout...
exit_conditions: Place target profits at the next opposite swing point...
risk_management: When entering a breakout, place a protective stop outside the breakout bar...
In the explosion gap pivot strategy, place protective stops initially at the gap low...
Avoid trading individual asset contract months that have low open interest...
Place protective stops for long positions just below round numbers...
market_structure: Prices move in trends that possess three distinct directions...
Dow Theory: A major trend is assumed to remain in effect...
Support is the price level where demand is strong enough...
Role Reversal: Broken support levels become resistance levels...
A valid trendline requires at least two points...
psychology: Market history tends to repeat itself...
Human cognitive biases create common investment dangers...
Support and resistance levels are psychologically created...
Round numbers act as major psychological support...
key_concepts: False Breakouts and Failed Breakout Traps...
